mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals labor market strength and sustained consumer demand, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 143,048 thousand indicates near-trend employment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite strong payroll growth, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month by > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 568,215M and HOUST at 1,580K suggest moderate demand
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint demand contraction, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production below 107.0 (index 2017=100) signals supply-side weakness and reduced capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 107.0 and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.1843 is near the threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 107.0 are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as discretionary spending and earnings decline.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.2% is below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE > 6.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint condition of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL growth > 3% annualized) and elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and increases discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 263.161 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% indicate low inflation and accommodation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite tight monetary conditions, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) below trend growth of 2.0% signals economic slowdown and reduced corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18,783.902 billion (2017 dollars) reflects recent economic activity
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with sub-2.0% GDP growth are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous surge in housing starts (HOUST > 1,700K) and retail sales growth (RSAFS > 2% month-over-month) signals robust consumer demand and forward economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings guidance improves.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,580K and RSAFS at 568,215M suggest moderate demand conditions
regime_hint: demand-driven expansion with strong consumer activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint demand strength, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression model.
